pr.title: Improve README accessibility and presentation with simplified language and visual enhancements
pr.body:
The README was difficult to parse due to unclear technical language, grammatical errors, and poor information hierarchy. This made the document inaccessible to non-expert readers.

## Changes

- **Simplified Technical Language**: Rewrote all explanations using clear, grade-school appropriate language without sacrificing technical accuracy. Replaced jargon with analogies (e.g., "sensor = thermometer for your computer's brain")

- **Added Visual Elements**: Incorporated emojis strategically to improve scannability and engagement throughout the document

- **Fixed Grammar & Spelling**: Corrected all spelling errors (mesruing → measuring, currectly → currently, pluglin → plugin, etc.) and improved sentence structure

- **Reorganized Structure**: Added clear section headings, logical flow progression (beginner → advanced), and better spacing for readability

- **Expanded Explanations**: Extended sections to provide comprehensive yet accessible explanations of complex concepts like kernel drivers, MSR registers, and the temperature calculation chain

The document is now suitable for readers of varying technical backgrounds while maintaining depth for advanced users.
pr.author: Copilot
